description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a predominantly deep blue coloration, interspersed with lighter white and gray marbling throughout its surface. The rock has a rugged, natural texture with visible creases, ridges, and subtle angular facets, giving it a weathered appearance. Its surface is mostly matte with some slightly smoother patches, suggesting exposure to the elements. The coloration and marbling pattern are reminiscent of sodalite or similar metamorphic stones, often used in decorative landscaping. The rock appears solid and dense, with no visible cavities or openings. Its size and form make it suitable as a natural barrier or decorative element in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A rugged, deep blue rock with white and gray marbling, featuring an irregular, weathered surface and angular facets. Its solid, dense form is visually striking and suitable for use as a natural barrier or landscape feature.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the rock, highlighting its deep blue color and prominent white marbling, with a slightly sloped top and rough surface texture.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a more elongated, vertical profile with pronounced ridges and a sharper edge, emphasizing the rock's irregular geometry.
description_view_2: The side view displays a relatively flat surface with subtle marbling and a blocky, angular silhouette, making the rock appear more massive.
description_view_3: This perspective shows a triangular face with a mix of dark blue and lighter patches, and a pronounced edge running from top to bottom.
attribute_location: urban park or landscaped public space
